title: Live auto-refresh: viewer updates when a wiki file changes
description:
FIRST STEP: 'git merge main --no-edit' in the worktree; verify the viewer + comment client exist.

Goal: when the agent edits a wiki .md (e.g. in response to a comment), the open browser view updates WITHOUT a manual reload — closing the human↔agent loop.

Server (kb.py): add a cheap change signal — include an ETag or mtime+size for the page in /api/page responses, and add GET /api/changed?path=<rel> returning the file's mtime (sandboxed like /api/page). Optionally GET /api/revision = max mtime across the wiki (for sidebar/status refresh).

Client (app.js): while a page is open, poll the change signal every ~1.5s; if the viewed file changed, re-fetch /api/page and re-render in place (preserve scroll position), and refresh its staleness badge. Periodically (or on revision change) refresh the sidebar tree + status pills too. Stdlib + polling only — NO SSE/websocket.

Touches kb.py (server) + app.js — runs after the comment client (1kk) to avoid app.js conflicts.

Test: open a page in the browser; edit its .md on disk; within ~2s the rendered content + badge update with no manual reload; scroll position preserved.
acceptance_criteria: Editing a viewed page's .md on disk auto-updates the rendered content + staleness badge within ~2s via polling (scroll preserved); sidebar/status refresh on change; stdlib only
closed_reason: Completed: /api/changed + /api/revision + mtime in /api/page (sandboxed, 400 on traversal); client polls 1.5s, re-renders open page in place (scroll preserved) on mtime bump, refreshes sidebar/status on revision change, pauses during comment modal/selection.
